task.title: relayfile login: fall back to cloud browser flow when no --token

events.content: When no --token is provided, runLogin will trigger ensureCloudCredentials (browser flow) instead of prompting for API key: When no --token is provided, runLogin will trigger ensureCloudCredentials (browser flow) instead of prompting for API key

raw.question: When no --token is provided, runLogin will trigger ensureCloudCredentials (browser flow) instead of prompting for API key
raw.chosen: When no --token is provided, runLogin will trigger ensureCloudCredentials (browser flow) instead of prompting for API key
raw.reasoning: Matches what 'relayfile setup' already does. Old API-key prompt path stays available behind --api-key flag for self-hosted users who don't have cloud.

retrospective.summary: relayfile login now defaults to the cloud browser flow when --token is absent; legacy API-key prompt is preserved behind --api-key. Adds --cloud-api-url/--cloud-token/--no-open/--login-timeout flags mirroring 'setup', plus 3 unit tests.
